summary: Two-story rectangular brick building with a dark stepped gable roof, stone/white trim base and cornices, horizontal wooden band between floors, regular rows of small glass windows, and corner stone pillars. Sits on a raised white slab border around a dirt foundation.

shape.overall_form: simple rectangular prism with a stepped gable roof
shape.symmetry: bilaterally symmetric along the long center axis (front/back mirrored)

elements: rows of small glass windows on both floors (repeating pattern)
entrance not visible (uncertain) - likely on opposite/side face
stepped dark gable roof with quartz cornice accents
raised quartz slab border foundation around perimeter
wooden horizontal band between first and second floor
rebuild_hints: Create a rectangular two-floor box of brick with corners reinforced with stonebrick columns (3-high corners visible).
Add a continuous oak plank band around mid-height between floors and place a quartz slab/cornice above and below the band.
Place repeating 1x1 or 1x2 glass windows in a regular row on each floor (spacing consistent across faces).
Construct a stepped dark nether_brick gable roof centered on the long axis, add quartz block caps at the eaves corners and small quartz slab highlights on steps.
Surround the building with a one-block-high quartz slab/blocks border raised above the dirt ground as shown.
uncertainties: Exact interior layout and presence/location of doors are not visible from provided exterior images.
Material IDs: quartz vs. smooth_sandstone or diorite could be used for white trim; confidence given but not certain.
Corner vertical blocks may be stonebrick or andesite; identification is medium confidence.
Exact block counts and precise footprint dimensions estimated from perspective/top views; actual counts may differ by a few blocks.
